Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_vectral", or dealname: "Vectral" or similar) in order to discover the at-risk renewal record, Rachel Whitfield's discount demand, and the client's stated grievances (stipend friction, ExpenseBot rejections, onboarding history) that underpin the negotiation.
- Search CRM companies and deals using crm_search_companies or crm_search_deals (name: "Terraform Digital", "Vantage Distributed" or similar) in order to confirm Terraform is the same account as legacy Vantage Distributed in older notes and capture deal economics for the onboarding and relocation batch so forecasts are not double-counted across duplicate naming.
- Pull CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: [ "company_vectral", "company-terraform-digital" ]) to gather narrative context that may not appear in deal amount fields alone.
- Search Airtable stipend transactions using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: the Stipend Transactions table discovered via airtable_list_tables, field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems", and other clients as needed) for stipend line items. The agent may first need to call airtable_list_tables to discover the correct table name or ID.
- Search Airtable client account records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: the Client Accounts table discovered via airtable_list_tables, field_name: "Client", value: "Vectral Systems", and other clients as needed) for contract-level notes. The agent may first need to call airtable_list_tables to discover the correct table name or ID.
- Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices and quickbooks_search_bills (criteria {"CustomerRef": "cust_vectral" }, fetchAll true or similar) to compare billed/revenue lines and vendor entries to Airtable and CRM figures; note any payment holds, contingent liabilities, or duplicate customer names that could inflate exposure.
- Search emails using search_emails (query: "Vectral", "Terraform" or similar) to align email narrative with CRM deal text and stipend tracker rows.
- Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Vectral", "Terraform" or similar) to align slack messages narrative with CRM deal text and stipend tracker rows.
- Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "ExpenseBot", "stipend", "ENG-216" or similar) to tie engineering-track remediation and audit findings to dollar exposure called out in stipend review notes.
- Look up contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim", "Alejandro Fuentes" or similar) to find the email addresses alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com and hana.kim@moveops.com to contact them via email.
- Send email using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: [alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com], cc: [hana.kim@moveops.com]) with a subject that signals "renewal and margin risk" and a body containing the full reconciliation memo, including: client account for Invoiced (QB), Stipend Paid, and Stipend Pending, double-counting, unexplained payout, fraud-adjacent noise, and the CRM deal notes.
- Send a message using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005) with a brief summary of the full reconciliation memo.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

9 The final response reports the current state of the discount dispute with Vectral Systems (e.g. Rachel demanded 15%; we countered 10% relocation-only; she countered 12%; we held at 10% with service sweeteners. Board decision expected around April 23. Or similar)
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

outcome

11 The final response flags three Vectral employees still waiting on wrongly rejected stipends: Maria Santos ($550 standing desk), James Okafor ($82 ergonomic keyboard tray), and Lisa Park ($130 mesh WiFi system), totaling approximately $762
